behaviors.description: SUM over a range evaluates correctly (`=SUM(A1:A10)`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a unit test of SUM alone can miss range parsing and cell-address resolution bugs. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking the cell grid could hide off-by-one range boundary failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 to known numeric values, set B1 to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run recalculation, and assert B1 equals the expected total including boundary cells A1 and A10.

behaviors.description: Cross-cell cascaded formulas evaluate correctly (`B1 = A1*2`, `C1 = B1+1`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: direct function tests miss dependency wiring between referenced formula cells. REAL-INFRA: no external infra required. MOCK-HIDE: mocking dependency resolution may always pass while real graph traversal fails.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=3, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1` in the same workbook; recalc and assert B1=6 and C1=7.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency is detected and surfaced as an error (A1↔B1).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: cycle detection is a property of the dependency graph across cells, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: isolated evaluator mocks can miss infinite traversal/cycle marking defects.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=`=B1+1` and B1=`=A1+1`, trigger recalculation, and assert both cells report circular-reference error without hang or unbounded recursion.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: function logic plus table-range extraction and key comparison semantics must work together. REAL-INFRA: no external systems. MOCK-HIDE: mocking lookup table access can hide column-index/range-shape bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a small lookup table in cells, evaluate `=VLOOKUP(key, range, colIndex, FALSE)` for an existing key and a missing key, and assert correct value and not-found error respectively.

behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: nested parsing + expression evaluation + branching across cell values can fail at module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: testing only IF helper logic can miss parser/AST nesting defects.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to representative values (e.g., 5, 15, 25), evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`, and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high`.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: empty-cell semantics depend on actual cell-value retrieval and coercion, not just string join logic. REAL-INFRA: no external infra. MOCK-HIDE: mocked value providers can mask null/empty distinctions.
behaviors.test_description: Populate cells with text and empties, evaluate a CONCATENATE formula using separators, and assert output has expected separators around non-empty values only, with empty cells omitted.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference resolves when target sheet exists (`=Sheet2!A1+1`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires workbook-level sheet namespace resolution plus formula evaluation. REAL-INFRA: no external systems needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking sheet lookup can hide sheet-name binding bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1=10, set Sheet1!B1=`=Sheet2!A1+1`, recalc, and assert Sheet1!B1=11.

behaviors.description: Dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order after source change.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering correctness is a dependency-graph engine behavior across multiple cells/modules. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing recalculation scheduler can hide stale-value propagation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond graph of formulas, compute baseline, change an upstream cell, trigger recalc, and assert all downstream cells reflect new values (no stale intermediates).

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and prior dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: spans editor state, undo stack, formula storage, and recalculation engine. REAL-INFRA: no external infra required. MOCK-HIDE: mocked undo/recalc components can miss state-synchronization defects.
behaviors.test_description: Set an initial formula with known dependent results, edit the formula to change outputs, invoke undo once, and assert original formula text and all affected dependent cell values are restored.

behaviors.description: Save/load round-trip preserves formulas, values, and floating-point precision.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: serializer/deserializer compatibility and precision retention require real binary read/write path. REAL-INFRA: uses local filesystem (fits Integration per level defs). MOCK-HIDE: mocked persistence can hide byte-format or numeric encoding loss.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with formulas and representative floating-point values, save to a temp file via real persistence code, reload it, and assert formulas and evaluated values match pre-save results within strict precision expectations.
